statement.en: What factors facilitate and hinder the embedding of JESIP into wider category one, category two, and third sector organisations. Report produced by the University of Liverpool for the Joint Emergency Services Interoperability Programme.
statement.summary: This report details the findings of a study undertaken by the University of Liverpool to examine what factors facilitate and hinder the embedding of JESIP within wider category one, category two and third sector organisations who have roles in responding to major incidents. Between April and July 2025, 26 semi-structured interviews were conducted with representatives from across a range of agencies. Key findings are as follows: - Participants perceived JESIP to be focused on blue light responders rather than seeking to include all agencies with a role in preparing for and responding to major incidents. Feedback points to the use of exclusionary language, use of only blue light examples in training materials, a focus on hierarchical structures, and lack of recognition regarding wider agency

proposer: Sara Waring, Nicole Balmer, Hannah Langley et al.
